# Storage Fibre Channel Port

**Status**: [Development][DocumentStatus]

This document defines semantic conventions for storage Fibre Channel ports.

## Storage FC Port Entity

A storage Fibre Channel (FC) port is a physical interface on a storage system that
provides high-speed connectivity using the Fibre Channel protocol.

<!-- semconv entity.storage.fc_port -->

| Identity | [`storage.fc_port.wwpn`](/docs/registry/attributes/storage.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` | string | Indicates the unique worldwide port name (WWPN) of the Fibre Channel port. | `50:05:07:68:01:23:45:67`; `21:00:00:24:FF:12:34:56` |
| Description | [`storage.fc_port.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/storage.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` | string | The logical name of the Fibre Channel port within the storage system. | `node1`; `FC PORT 1`; `fc0` |
| Description | [`storage.fc_port.status`](/docs/registry/attributes/storage.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` | string | Indicates the status of the specific Fibre Channel port. | `operational`; `offline`; `error` |
